name: Ginger Beer
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the water in a stainless steel or enamel pot.
2. Add all remaining ingredients except the yeast to the boiling water and simmer for 20 minutes.
3. Cool the mixture to lukewarm and taste. Adjust the amount of ginger if desired for a livelier flavor.
4. Dissolve the yeast in one cup of the cooled mixture.
5. Stir the dissolved yeast back into the main brew.
6. Cover and allow to ferment at 65-70°F (18-21°C) for 34-36 hours.
7. After fermentation, siphon or pour the mixture into sterilized bottles, removing any lemon slices.
8. Cap the bottles tightly and store them upright for one week at 60-65°F (15-18°C).
9. Transfer the bottled ginger beer to the refrigerator or a cool basement room below 60°F (15.5°C) for storage.
